Title: Expression of AtWRI1 and AtDGAT1 during soybean embryo development influences oil and carbohydrate metabolism

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Glyma.05G004300; Glyma.19G004800; GmOLE1 cited as Glyma.20G196600
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Putative oleosin genes were strongly down-regulated during seed development in WD events
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AtWRI1 and AtDGAT1 co-expression during embryo development
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; association with unchanged TAG accumulation despite activation of FA synthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced oil-body packaging capacity is proposed to limit oil accumulation in transgenic embryos
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Oleosin gene expression was markedly reduced in WD embryos. 2. Reduced oil-body structural protein abundance likely restricted TAG packaging/storage despite induction of FA synthesis machinery. 3. Oil-body packaging capacity is highlighted as a bottleneck for soybean oil engineering.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Co-engineering WRI1/DGAT with oleosin or other lipid-droplet packaging genes may be necessary to achieve higher seed oil.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors cite previous work showing soybean oil content may be limited by oleosin expression level.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated during seed development in WD events
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AtWRI1 and AtDGAT1 co-expression during embryo development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to impaired oil-body stabilization and limited seed oil accumulation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Oil-body structure/stability associated with storage lipid deposition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Caleosin transcripts were strongly reduced in WD embryos. 2. Down-regulation of lipid-droplet packaging proteins coincided with failure to increase TAG content. 3. Packaging/stabilization functions appear relevant constraints in oil engineering.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive targets for stacking with oil-promoting regulators and enzymes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because role is structural packaging rather than catalytic or transcriptional.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated during development in WD events; one gene identified among vetted putative AtWRI1 targets
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AtWRI1 and AtDGAT1 co-expression during embryo development
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; AW-box motif/correlation for Glyma.18G023300
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to inhibit plastidic ACC and contribute to restriction of fatty acid accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two BADC genes were induced in WD embryos. 2. BADCs are proposed pACC inhibitors and may represent a bottleneck counteracting WRI1-driven FA synthesis. 3. Their induction exemplifies homeostatic feedback limiting uncontrolled oil increase.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate targets for AW-box editing or down-regulation to protect oil gain in WRI1-based engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note literature conflict regarding whether BADC inhibits or assists ACC complex assembly; role needs further validation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL esterase/lipase; monoacylglycerol lipase; alpha/beta hydrolase lipolytic proteins
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly up-regulated during seed development in WD events, with some genes induced up to 64-fold; several are putative AtWRI1 targets
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AtWRI1 and AtDGAT1 co-expression during embryo development
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; AW-box motif and co-expression analysis for selected genes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes lipid turnover/futile cycling and restricts net oil accumulation in transgenic soybean seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple lipase genes were induced in WD embryos, including three GDSL lipases and one MAGL-like gene highlighted as putative AtWRI1 targets. 2. Their induction provides a plausible mechanism for futile FA/TAG cycling that prevents oil increase. 3. Lipolytic targets are key negative factors to consider in soybean oil engineering.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidates for targeted down-regulation or promoter/AW-box editing when stacking WRI1-based oil traits.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors interpret these genes as part of a homeostatic rheostat that counters excessive TAG accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TAG assembly, Kennedy pathway terminal acylation step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Final and rate-limiting step of triacylglycerol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage oil and nutritional vegetable oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed during embryo development; lower transcript accumulation than AtWRI1 across reproductive stages analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic co-expression in soybean; RNA-seq detection across seed development; mature seed lipid/TAG composition analysis; developmental biomass profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression with AtWRI1 did not increase total fatty acids or TAG content, but altered fatty acid composition and seed carbon partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed yield; reduced germination; wrinkled-seed phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDGAT1 was successfully expressed in developing soybean embryos under a seed-specific promoter. 2. Although DGAT1 catalyzes the final rate-limiting TAG biosynthetic step, its expression together with AtWRI1 failed to increase TAG or total FA accumulation. 3. The stack altered seed fatty acid composition and carbon partitioning, implying downstream constraints on oil deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful TAG assembly gene for stacking, but alone with AtWRI1 is insufficient in soybean Thorne; future engineering should combine with oil-body packaging support and suppression of competing/lipolytic pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtDGAT1 transcript levels were about 10-fold lower than AtWRI1 despite use of the same beta-conglycinin promoter, which may have limited phenotypic impact.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acyl-ACP chain termination, saturated fatty acid release branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes acyl-ACP thioesters to release free fatty acids from plastid fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Palmitic acid / seed fatty acid composition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Component of storage oil composition and nutritional oil quality determinant
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing and mature soybean seed oil fraction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated across developmental stages in transgenic events; identified among vetted putative AtWRI1 targets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AtWRI1 and AtDGAT1 transgene expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; AW-box motif analysis; correlation with AtWRI1 expression; association with fatty acid composition phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to increased palmitic acid and decreased stearic/oleic acid levels in transgenic soybean seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma.04G151600 was consistently up-regulated in WD events and contains an AW-box, supporting it as a putative AtWRI1 target. 2. Its induction was linked to higher palmitate and lower stearate/oleate in developing and mature seeds. 3. FATB may underlie oil composition shifts rather than increased total oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target when engineering oil quality; should be monitored or edited to avoid undesirable palmitate increase during WRI1-based oil engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes prior contrasting evidence from another soybean WRI1 overexpression study, so contribution of individual FATB homologs needs further testing.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of fatty acid and glycolysis genes; indirect repression/downstream negative autoregulatory effect on endogenous WRI1 homologs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes of plastid pyruvate kinase; pyruvate dehydrogenase; heteromeric ACC subunits; ketoacyl-ACP synthase I/III; hydroxyacyl-ACP dehydratase; enoyl-ACP reductase; GmFATB (Glyma.04G151600); UGP2 (Glyma.13G152500); GPT2-like gene (Glyma.17G015500); lipases including Glyma.09G074000; Glyma.15G182900; Glyma.15G183000; MAGL-like Glyma.06G087100; BADC genes Glyma.11G233700 and Glyma.18G023300
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Up-regulates glycolysis and de novo fatty acid synthesis genes and multiple putative lipolytic/inhibitory genes; associated with down-regulation of endogenous GmWRI1 homologs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis, glycolysis/carbon flux, branch selection and homeostatic limitation of oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative autoregulatory loop inferred: strong AtWRI1 expression associated with down-regulation of endogenous GmWRI1 homologs; trehalose-6-phosphate/WRI1 stability discussed as upstream metabolic regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed developmental program; soybean beta-conglycinin promoter; trehalose-6-phosphate/sucrose status discussed as modulator of WRI1 stability
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High transgene expression during embryo development, approximately 10-fold higher than AtDGAT1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and Nebraska field conditions during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in soybean; RNA-seq across developmental stages; comparative transcriptomics with W-1 (AtWRI1-only) and WD events; AW-box motif vetting; Pearson co-expression analysis; metabolite and seed composition profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Changed seed carbon partitioning and fatty acid composition without increasing total oil; promoted accumulation of soluble sugars/starch and induced pathways restricting excess oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed yield and germination; wrinkled seeds; possible root-expression-related effects from promoter choice; induction of lipases and FA biosynthesis inhibitors may counter oil gain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional seed oil and storage energy reserve
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRI1 was robustly expressed in developing soybean embryos and activated many canonical glycolytic and fatty-acid-biosynthetic targets. 2. Despite this push toward oil synthesis, total FA and TAG did not increase because carbon was diverted to starch/cell wall pathways and homeostatic countermeasures were triggered. 3. Comparative expression, AW-box screening, and correlation analysis identified 82 putative soybean AtWRI1 targets, including beneficial biosynthetic genes and undesirable lipases/BADC inhibitors.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful seed-oil engineering regulator, but should be paired with strategies that enhance oil-body packaging, curb lipolysis and BADC-like inhibition, and reduce competing starch/cell-wall sinks; promoter choice is critical.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous GmWRI1 homologs Glyma.08G227700 and Glyma.15G221600 were down-regulated in WD embryos; paper emphasizes this may reduce recognition of soybean-specific targets not fully substituted by AtWRI1.
